name: Alert the team on new events with Twilio 24/7 for Fulfillment

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09154-order`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Map to Canonical Schema

### Map to Canonical Schema

Shapes the item: writes `orderId`, `kind`, `payload`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Route by Event Kind

### Route by Event Kind

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: opened, closed, reopened + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Handle 'opened' → Google Sheets · ②→ Handle 'closed' → Forward API · ③→ Handle 'reopened' → Forward API · ④→ Log Unrecognized Kind

### Handle 'opened' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Order Tracker', matched on `orderId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Handle 'closed' → Forward API

POST https://api.example.com/v1/orders — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Handle 'reopened' → Forward API

POST https://api.example.com/v1/orders — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Log Unrecognized Kind

Appends a new row to 'Order Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Consolidate Handling

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Respond with Outcome

### Respond with Outcome

Sends the HTTP 200 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

# Alert the team on new events with Twilio 24/7 for Fulfillment

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Alert the team on new events with Twilio 24/7 for Fulfillment. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual fulfillment is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Map to Canonical Schema → Route by Event Kind → Handle 'opened' → Google Sheets → Consolidate Handling → Respond with Outcome

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating fulfillment in e-commerce
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Improves response time
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The optional HMAC check runs before any business logic touches the payload — bad signatures are rejected at the door rather than after work has already been done with untrusted data. Acking fast (HTTP 202) and doing the real work after the response is what stops the sender's webhook delivery from timing out and re-delivering the same event.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Turn on signature verification and trace what a tampered payload does at the 'Signature Valid?' gate.
2. Add a new event kind to the switch and give it its own handler.
3. Remove the fast-ack step and observe how response time changes under a slow downstream write.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your fulfillment process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
